title: Boston 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Boston 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Boston, United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Museum of Fine Arts, Boston and New England Aquarium. Prices from $2321 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Boston, United States — Humid continental climate with four distinct seasons: warm, humid summers (highs 27-29°C) and cold, snowy winters (highs 2-4°C, frequent nor'easter snowstorms), bookended by a particularly picturesque autumn. September to November for crisp air, comfortable highs (15-20°C) and spectacular New England fall foliage; January-March brings cold, snowy winters (highs often near or below freezing). This itinerary blends Museum of Fine Arts, Boston, New England Aquarium, Freedom Trail with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Museum of Fine Arts, Boston, New England Aquarium, Freedom Trail
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Boston
itinerary.description: Land at Logan International Airport (BOS) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Museum of Fine Arts, Boston — One of the largest and most comprehensive art museums in the US. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Boston: New England Aquarium & Fenway Park tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to New England Aquarium — Waterfront aquarium with a giant ocean tank and penguins. In the afternoon, continue to Fenway Park tour — Behind-the-scenes tour of America's oldest active Major League Baseball park. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Boston: USS Constitution Museum & Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to USS Constitution Museum — Museum beside the world's oldest commissioned warship still afloat. In the afternoon, continue to Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum — Venetian-style palazzo museum famed for an unsolved 1990 art heist. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Salem
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Salem, a popular side trip from Boston. Return to Boston in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Acorn Street, Beacon Hill — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Boston: Skywalk Observatory, Prudential Center & Boston Duck Tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Skywalk Observatory, Prudential Center — 360-degree observation deck over the city. In the afternoon, continue to Boston Duck Tour — Amphibious vehicle tour covering both land and Charles River. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Cambridge (Harvard & MIT)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Cambridge (Harvard & MIT), a popular side trip from Boston. Return to Boston in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Boston: Freedom Trail & Boston Public Garden
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Freedom Trail — 2.5-mile red-brick path connecting 16 Revolutionary War-era historic sites. In the afternoon, continue to Boston Public Garden — Historic botanical garden with the famous swan boats. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Boston Athenaeum reading rooms — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Cape Cod
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Cape Cod, a popular side trip from Boston. Return to Boston in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Boston: Quincy Market / Faneuil Hall Marketplace & Harvard Yard, Cambridge
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Quincy Market / Faneuil Hall Marketplace — Historic market hall with food stalls and street performers. In the afternoon, continue to Harvard Yard, Cambridge — Historic heart of Harvard University's campus. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Boston: Charles River Esplanade & Beacon Hill
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Charles River Esplanade — Riverside park popular for jogging and picnicking. In the afternoon, continue to Beacon Hill — Gas-lit, cobblestone historic residential neighborhood. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Arnold Arboretum — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to North End or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to BOS for your departure flight.

travelTips: Walk the Freedom Trail's red-brick line at your own pace — it's free and self-guided, though official guided tours add historical depth.
Visit in late September to mid-October for peak fall foliage, one of the best times to visit New England overall.
Book a Fenway Park tour even outside baseball season — it runs nearly year-round.
Cambridge (Harvard/MIT) is a quick T ride across the river and worth a half-day on its own.
The Boston Marathon (Patriots' Day, April) causes major road closures — check the route if visiting that week.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
A heavy coat and waterproof boots for snowy winters
Comfortable walking shoes for the cobblestones of Beacon Hill and the Freedom Trail
Layers for changeable New England weather in spring/fall
thingsToDo: Kayaking or sailing on the Charles River
Whale watching boat tours from Boston Harbor
Cycling the Charles River Esplanade path
Harbor Islands ferry and hiking day trip
Swan boats in the Public Garden
thingsToAvoid: Driving downtown — Boston's street layout is notoriously confusing and parking is expensive; use the T instead.
Underestimating winter cold and nor'easter snowstorms — check forecasts before finalizing a winter itinerary.
Skipping reservations at top North End restaurants on weekend evenings — waits can run over an hour.
Walking on the Charles River Esplanade path against cyclist traffic — it's a shared but directional path.
Assuming all historic sites are open the same hours — some Freedom Trail stops have seasonal or limited hours.
topRestaurants: Union Oyster House — Seafood, oldest restaurant in continuous service in the US ($$$)
Regina Pizzeria, North End — Italian Pizza ($)
Legal Sea Foods — New England Seafood ($$$)
nightlife: Bell in Hand Tavern (oldest tavern in the US)
instagramSpots: Acorn Street, Beacon Hill (a famously photogenic cobblestone lane)
familyActivities: Swan boats in the Public Garden
adventureActivities: Kayaking or sailing on the Charles River
Whale watching boat tours from Boston Harbor
Cycling the Charles River Esplanade path
Harbor Islands ferry and hiking day trip
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at the Boston Harbor Hotel
Private Freedom Trail tour with a historian guide
Fine dining at a North End tasting-menu restaurant
honeymoonExperiences: Swan boat ride in the Public Garden
Sunset walk along the Charles River Esplanade
Romantic dinner in the North End
budgetExperiences: Free Boston Public Garden and Common visit

festivalCalendar.name: Boston Marathon
festivalCalendar.timing: Patriots' Day, third Monday of April

festivalCalendar.name: Fourth of July Boston Pops concert & fireworks

localEtiquette: Tipping 18-20% at restaurants and for services is standard.
Bostonians are proud and vocal about their sports teams (Red Sox, Celtics, Patriots, Bruins) — friendly banter is welcome, mockery less so.
The "T" subway (America's oldest) has its own local rhythm — stand right on escalators, let riders off before boarding.
Ivy League campus visits (Harvard, MIT) are generally welcoming to respectful visitors during open hours.
localTransportGuide: The MBTA "T" subway is the oldest in the US and covers downtown, Cambridge and most tourist sites well; the city is also highly walkable, especially along the Freedom Trail. Uber/Lyft and the Charles River water shuttle round out the options.
bestTimeToVisit: September to November for crisp air, comfortable highs (15-20°C) and spectacular New England fall foliage; January-March brings cold, snowy winters (highs often near or below freezing).
weather: Humid continental climate with four distinct seasons: warm, humid summers (highs 27-29°C) and cold, snowy winters (highs 2-4°C, frequent nor'easter snowstorms), bookended by a particularly picturesque autumn.
visaInformation: Citizens of Visa Waiver Program countries can enter with an approved ESTA for stays up to 90 days. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, require a B1/B2 visitor visa obtained in advance via a US embassy/consulate interview — a common route given the large number of Indian students and academic visitors to Boston-area universities.
averageFlightTime: ~13.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Boston?
faqs.answer: Citizens of Visa Waiver Program countries can enter with an approved ESTA for stays up to 90 days. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, require a B1/B2 visitor visa obtained in advance via a US embassy/consulate interview — a common route given the large number of Indian students and academic visitors to Boston-area universities.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Boston?
faqs.answer: September to November for crisp air, comfortable highs (15-20°C) and spectacular New England fall foliage; January-March brings cold, snowy winters (highs often near or below freezing). Humid continental climate with four distinct seasons: warm, humid summers (highs 27-29°C) and cold, snowy winters (highs 2-4°C, frequent nor'easter snowstorms), bookended by a particularly picturesque autumn.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Boston safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Boston is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Boston?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Elena Cohen
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond New England Aquarium and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Olivia Kowalski
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Our trip to Boston was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Charles River Esplanade was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Grace Kim
